document_metadata.title: 25 death notices in Stoke-on-Trent and North Staffordshire this week
document_metadata.first_words: These are the death notices published in this week's editions of The Sentinel. Our thoughts are with the families and friends of their loved-ones. You can see the latest listings from your area on the InYourArea section. To see the full list of family announcements, visit this section of the StokeonTrentLive website. chunks.content: Peacefully at rest on 10th August 2025, in the wonderful care of the Dougie Mac, Dave, aged 55 years, of Barlaston. Beloved husband of Helen, devoted father to Emily and Lizzie, and cherished father-in-law to Jacqui. A much-loved brother to Wendy and Diane, son-in-law to Peter and Christine, also a cherished uncle, nephew, brother-in-law, and a dear friend and colleague to many. Dave will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him. The family wish to express their heartfelt thanks to all the staff at the Dougie Mac Hospice for the outstanding care and compassion shown to Dave and his family.

chunks.content: It is with great sadness that we announce the passing of Carol Reeves, a beloved wife, mother, Nan and great nan, who left us peacefully on 13th August 2025, aged 65 years. Carol was a devoted and caring wife to Stuart, whose unwavering love and companionship were the foundation of her family. As a mother, she gave boundlessly of herself-guiding with wisdom, nurturing with kindness, and celebrating every joy in her children's lives. As a nan, she brought endless warmth, comfort, and laughter, leaving behind cherished memories that will be held close to the hearts of her grandchildren and great grandchild. Her gentle spirit, selfless love, and bright presence touched everyone who knew her. She will be remembered for her generosity, her strength, and her ability to make each person feel deeply loved. Though she is no longer with us, her legacy lives on in the family she nurtured and the lives she touched. She was our everything, and is missed more than words can say. Gone but never forgotten, forever in our hearts.

As a mother, she gave boundlessly of herself-guiding with wisdom, nurturing with kindness, and celebrating every joy in her children's lives. As a nan, she brought endless warmth, comfort, and laughter, leaving behind cherished memories that will be held close to the hearts of her grandchildren and great grandchild. Her gentle spirit, selfless love, and bright presence touched everyone who knew her. She will be remembered for her generosity, her strength, and her ability to make each person feel deeply loved. Though she is no longer with us, her legacy lives on in the family she nurtured and the lives she touched. She was our everything, and is missed more than words can say.
